gaps: A per-era or per-Term base-rate cut keyed to the case's own filing date (this snapshot has no dates, so no --term/--date filter was usable)
A snapshot completeness indicator (or pre-filter) so near-empty legacy dockets are visible before prediction
Semantic/caption-based retrieval in fedcourts query to find same-era priors (structured filters alone rank heterogeneous historical records poorly)
helpful: fedcourts stats --court scotus (base-rate anchor for a fact-free snapshot)
fedcourts query --court scotus (showed the resolved corpus is mostly historical merits decisions labeled 'other')
notes: The snapshot carried no case facts, so the corpus tooling did all the work: base rates plus a look at resolved priors drove the disposition call. The binary granted/probability contract forces a cert-style frame onto a writ-of-error-era case; see flags.json.
